page: Amazon Web Services c9gd.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs on an arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, meaning there is no hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This hardware configuration is paired with 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. This high-capacity local storage provides low-latency, high-throughput data access directly on the physical host.

Built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor, the c9gd.48xlarge delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The Nitro architecture offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware, maximizing the resources available to the guest operating system.

Given the compute-heavy profile with a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and extensive local NVMe SSD storage, this instance is designed for workloads requiring high-density arm64 compute and fast local scratch space. Suitable applications include large-scale databases, data analytics, batch processing, and high-performance computing workloads that benefit from localized storage and high network throughput. The custom Graviton architecture offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage by delivering high core density and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of traditional x86 licensing.
description: Amazon Web Services c9gd.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring high-density parallel processing and fast local storage, such as distributed databases, data analytics, and batch processing. The Graviton architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for arm64-native applications.

meta_description: Amazon Web Services c9gd.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 192 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11.4 TB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A high-density arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, extensive local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking.

384.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio.
11,400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
